scenes.synopsis: Della counts the $1.87 she has saved — pennies at a time — and weeps at how little it buys for Jim's gift.
scenes.scriptText:
INT. DELLA & JIM'S FLAT - DAY

A shabby eight-dollar-a-week furnished flat. DELLA YOUNG, 22, counts a small hoard of coins on a worn coverlet.

DELLA
One dollar and eighty-seven cents. And the next day would be Christmas.

She flops onto the shabby little couch and howls.

scenes.synopsis: Della lets down her famous knee-length hair before the pier glass and resolves to sell it.
scenes.scriptText:
INT. FLAT - BEDROOM ALCOVE - DAY

Della stands before a cheap pier glass and lets down her hair — a shimmering cascade to below her knee.

DELLA (V.O.)
Her hair was her one glory.

elements.name: Hair-fall reveal
elements.description: Blend practical fall to actor's line

scenes.synopsis: Della hurries out into the grey winter street, coat clutched, toward the hair shop.
scenes.scriptText:
EXT. TENEMENT STREET - DAY

Della hurries down a slush-grey street, cheeks bright. A NEWSBOY hawks papers. She turns a corner beneath a swinging sign.

scenes.synopsis: Madame Sofronie appraises and buys Della's hair for twenty dollars. The shears fall.
scenes.scriptText:
INT. MME. SOFRONIE'S HAIR EMPORIUM - DAY

MADAME SOFRONIE, large, cold, weighs Della's loosened hair in one hand.

SOFRONIE
Twenty dollars.

DELLA
Give it to me quick.

SNIP. The shears close.

elements.name: Barber shears
elements.description: Period, sprung steel — SFX for the snip

elements.name: Hair-cut effect
elements.description: Actor keeps hair; sell the cut

scenes.synopsis: Della finds THE gift — a simple platinum fob chain for Jim's heirloom watch.
scenes.scriptText:
INT. JEWELER'S SHOP - DAY

A quiet, gleaming counter. The JEWELER lays out a platinum fob chain, simple and chaste.

DELLA
That one. For his watch.

scenes.synopsis: Home again, Della curls her cropped hair into tiny ringlets and starts the coffee.
scenes.scriptText:
INT. FLAT - DAY (LATER)

Della wields the curling irons on her cropped head, making close-lying little curls. She sets coffee to boil.

DELLA
Please God, make him think I am still pretty.

scenes.synopsis: Jim comes home, freezes in the doorway at the sight of Della's shorn hair.
scenes.scriptText:
INT. FLAT - DUSK

The door opens. JIM YOUNG, 22, thin and burdened in a coat that needs replacing, stops still. His eyes fix on Della — an expression she cannot read.

DELLA
Jim, darling, don't look at me that way.

scenes.synopsis: The gifts are exchanged: Jim's tortoise-shell combs for hair now gone; Della's fob for the watch Jim has sold to buy the combs.
scenes.scriptText:
INT. FLAT - NIGHT

Jim tosses a package on the table. Inside: THE COMBS — pure tortoise shell, jeweled rims — the set Della worshipped in a Broadway window.

DELLA
My hair grows so fast, Jim!

She presses the fob into his palm. Jim smiles, and sinks onto the couch.

JIM
I sold the watch to get your combs.

scenes.synopsis: INSERT — snow settles over the gas-lit street as the flat's window glows. Aerial pull-up.
scenes.scriptText:
EXT. TENEMENT STREET - NIGHT (INSERT)

Snow settling. Gas lamps haloed. We rise — an aerial pull-up — from the one warm window in a cold city.

scenes.synopsis: They put their gifts away and sit to supper. NARRATOR closes on the wisdom of the magi.
scenes.scriptText:
INT. FLAT - NIGHT

They put their presents away and Jim starts the chops on the fire.

NARRATOR (V.O.)
Of all who give gifts, these two were wisest. They are the magi.

characterBible.name: Della Young
characterBible.description: The wife. Sells her hair to buy Jim a gift.

characterBible.name: Jim Young
characterBible.description: The husband. Sells his watch to buy Della's combs.

notifications.body: NorthStar Aerial — drone day, AED 5,600. Awaiting accountant.

notifications.body: Tenement Street permit is blocked by neighbour sign-off.

notifications.title: Company move added — Day 2
notifications.body: Hair Emporium → Jeweler's Shop move at 11:00.

notifications.title: Daily digest ready
notifications.body: 3 shoot days scheduled · 2 POs pending · wig fitting in review.

aiDigest.text: Day 1 (flat interiors) is locked and camera-prepped. Two POs are pending — the aerial day and the snow package — both tied to the Day-3 night. The knee-length hair fall cleared to Ready, but the EXT night permit is blocked on a neighbour sign-off; clearing it is the critical path to the aerial pull-up (sc. 8A). Budget is tracking at 100% committed against a AED 272,000 plan.

roles.label: Administrator
roles.description: Full oversight of every project, department, users, roles, and AI.

roles.label: 1st AD / Scheduler
roles.description: Owns the shooting schedule, strip board, and DOOD.

roles.label: Accountant
roles.description: Manages the budget, POs, invoices, and petty cash.

roles.label: Camera / Technical
roles.description: Equipment manifests, kit builds, and prep checklists.

roles.label: VFX Supervisor
roles.description: Shot pipeline, vendor management, plate delivery.

roles.label: Art / Wardrobe / Props
roles.description: Element tracking, continuity, and set dressing.

roles.label: Cast Coordinator
roles.description: Cast schedules, DOOD, contracts, and call sheets.

projects.name: The Gift of the Magi
projects.logline: A young couple, too poor for Christmas, each sell their dearest possession to gift the other — O. Henry, 1905.

script.rawText:
INT. DELLA & JIM'S FLAT - DAY

A shabby eight-dollar-a-week furnished flat. DELLA YOUNG, 22, counts a small hoard of coins on a worn coverlet.

DELLA
One dollar and eighty-seven cents. And the next day would be Christmas.

She flops onto the shabby little couch and howls.

INT. FLAT - BEDROOM ALCOVE - DAY

Della stands before a cheap pier glass and lets down her hair — a shimmering cascade to below her knee.

DELLA (V.O.)
Her hair was her one glory.

EXT. TENEMENT STREET - DAY

Della hurries down a slush-grey street, cheeks bright. A NEWSBOY hawks papers. She turns a corner beneath a swinging sign.

INT. MME. SOFRONIE'S HAIR EMPORIUM - DAY

Twenty dollars.

Give it to me quick.

SNIP. The shears close.

INT. JEWELER'S SHOP - DAY

A quiet, gleaming counter. The JEWELER lays out a platinum fob chain, simple and chaste.

DELLA
That one. For his watch.

INT. FLAT - DAY (LATER)

Della wields the curling irons on her cropped head, making close-lying little curls. She sets coffee to boil.

DELLA
Please God, make him think I am still pretty.

INT. FLAT - DUSK

The door opens. JIM YOUNG, 22, thin and burdened in a coat that needs replacing, stops still. His eyes fix on Della — an expression she cannot read.

DELLA
Jim, darling, don't look at me that way.

INT. FLAT - NIGHT

Jim tosses a package on the table. Inside: THE COMBS — pure tortoise shell, jeweled rims — the set Della worshipped in a Broadway window.

DELLA
My hair grows so fast, Jim!

She presses the fob into his palm. Jim smiles, and sinks onto the couch.

I sold the watch to get your combs.

EXT. TENEMENT STREET - NIGHT (INSERT)

Snow settling. Gas lamps haloed. We rise — an aerial pull-up — from the one warm window in a cold city.

INT. FLAT - NIGHT

They put their presents away and Jim starts the chops on the fire.

NARRATOR (V.O.)
Of all who give gifts, these two were wisest. They are the magi.
